title: chaenorhinum minus subsp. minus
intro: Chaenorhinum minus subsp. minus, also known as the lesser toadflax or small toadflax, is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Chaenorhinum minus subsp. minus include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its possible use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, with the flowers and leaves being the parts most commonly used for medicinal purposes.
